revision.text: '''津守王子'''（つもりおうじ）は[[九十九王子]]の6番目。[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]墨江2―3の墨江小学校にあったが現在は姿を留めていない。現在は[[住吉大社]]境内の摂社・新宮社に合祀されたとも、[[住吉区]]沢之町1-10-4の止々呂岐比売神社に合祀されたともいわれている。

'''津守王子'''（'''つもりおうじ'''）は、[[九十九王子]]の6番目。[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]墨江2―3の墨江小学校にあったが現在は姿を留めていない。現在は[[住吉大社]]境内の摂社・新宮社に合祀されたとも、[[住吉区]]沢之町1-10-4の止々呂岐比売神社に合祀されたともいわれている。
熊野御幸をした天皇、上皇たちは、この地の[[住吉大社]]に和歌を奉げた。
*墨江小学校、住吉大社の最寄駅 [[南海電鉄]][[南海本線]][[住吉大社駅]]
*止々呂岐比売神社の最寄駅 [[南海電鉄]][[高野線]][[沢之町駅]]
[[Category:神道|つもりおうし]][[Category:大阪府|つもりおうし]] {{substub}}

'''津守王子'''（'''つもりおうじ'''）は、[[九十九王子]]の6番目。[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]墨江2-3の墨江小学校にあったが現在は姿を留めていない。現在は[[住吉大社]]境内の摂社・新宮社に合祀されたとも、住吉区沢之町1-10-4の[[止々呂岐比売神社]]（若松神社）に合祀されたともいわれている。
津守という名称は、住吉大社の古代よりの奉祭氏族であり宮司家でもあった[[津守氏]]にちなんだものである。 熊野御幸をした天皇、上皇たちは、この地の住吉大社に和歌を奉げた。 *墨江小学校、住吉大社の最寄駅　[[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海本線]][[住吉大社駅]] *止々呂岐比売神社の最寄駅　南海電鉄[[南海高野線|高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|つもりおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|つもりおうし]]

熊野御幸をした天皇、上皇たちは、この地の住吉大社に和歌を奉げた。

*墨江小学校、住吉大社の最寄駅 [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海本線]][[住吉大社駅]] *止々呂岐比売神社の最寄駅 南海電鉄[[南海高野線|高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] *: [[安倍野王子]] - '''津守王子''' - [[境王子]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つもりおうし}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:住吉区の歴史]]

'''津守王子'''（'''つもりおうじ'''）は、[[九十九王子]]の6番目の王子である。
[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]墨江2-3の[[大阪市立墨江小学校|墨江小学校]]にあったが現在は姿を留めていない。現在は[[住吉大社]]境内の摂社・新宮社に合祀されたとも、住吉区沢之町1-10-4の[[止止呂支比賣命神社|止々呂岐比売神社]]（若松神社）に合祀されたともいわれている。
津守という名称は、住吉大社の古代よりの奉斎氏族であり宮司家でもあった[[津守氏]]にちなんだものである。
熊野御幸をした天皇、上皇たちは、この地の住吉大社に和歌を奉げた。
{{右| [[ファイル:住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG|thumb|240px|none|[[住吉大社]]内王子社。旧[[津守王子]]。]] }}
*墨江小学校、住吉大社の最寄駅 [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海本線]][[住吉大社駅]] *止々呂岐比売神社の最寄駅 南海電鉄[[南海高野線|高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] *: [[安倍野王子]] - '''津守王子''' - [[境王子]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つもりおうし}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:住吉区の歴史]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

[[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Tsumori Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]]
[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

[[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[en:Tsumori Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]]
[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | Image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | Location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsumori Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] ]] [[ja:津守王子]][[zh:津守王子]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * **Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:** Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * **Totoro Kibime Shrine:** Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]
