revision.comment: adding [[Category:new pages not of uncertain originality]] to mark these ones
revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] - '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|九十九王子" to "|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji"
revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abeno Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (つもりおうじ) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - [[Nankai Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|ja|南海高野線}}: [[Sawanocho Station]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11555995]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11555995]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
