revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11555995]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11555995]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]]
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]]
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway|1=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line|1=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]]
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway|1=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line|1=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:津守王子]]
'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
== Access == * '''Sumie Elementary School / Sumiyoshi Taisha:''' Nearby station - {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway|1=Nankai Electric Railway}} {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}}: [[Sumiyoshi Taisha Station]] * '''Totoro Kibime Shrine:''' Nearby station - Nankai Electric Railway {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Koya Line|1=Koya Line}}: [[:en:Sawanocho Station|Sawanocho Station]]
== See also == * {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏}} * [[Kumano Faith]] * [[Kumano Kōdō]] - [[Kii Route]] * [[Kumano Ōji]] *: [[Abe Ōji Shrine|Abeno Ōji]] * '''Tsumori Ōji''' - [[Sakai Ōji]]

'''Tsumori Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tsumori Ōji | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Taisha Naioji Shrine (former Tsumori Ōji).JPG|240px|none|Former '''Tsumori Ōji''' within [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] grounds.]] | location = [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = |image = 住吉大社内王子社（旧津守王子）.JPG | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }}
'''Tsumori Ōji''' (津守王子) was the 6th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical pilgrimage routes leading to [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]].
Originally located at the site of today's [[Sumie Elementary School]] in [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], it no longer retains its original form. The shrine is believed to have been merged either with the {{ill|Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja|新宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingū Shrine (Sumiyoshi Taisha)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a sub-shrine within the precincts of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]], or with {{ill|Todoroki-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|止止呂支比賣命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totoro Kibime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 止止呂支比売命神社}} (also known as Wakamatsu Shrine) located at 1-10-4 Sawanocho, Sumiyoshi Ward.
The name "Tsumori" refers to the {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, an ancient family historically responsible for worship at Sumiyoshi Taisha and serving as its chief priests. Emperors and retired emperors on their {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} traditionally offered waka poems at this location.
